# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: redis-master-configmap
data:
  redis.conf: |
    port 6379
    requirepass <PASSWORD>
    masterauth <PASSWORD>
    min-slaves-to-write 0

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis-master
spec:
  serviceName: redis-master
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis-master
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: redis-master
    spec:
      containers:
      - image: redis
        name: redis-master
        ports:
          - containerPort: 6379
            protocol: TCP
        volumeMounts:
          - mountPath: /redis/data
            name: redis-master-data
        envFrom:
          - configMapRef:
              name: redis-master-configmap
      volumes:
      - name: redis-master-data
        emptyDir: {}

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis-master
spec:
  ports:
    - port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: redis-master
